perturbation.original_text: 1.1 “Affiliate ” means an affiliate as defined in the Business Corporations Act (British Columbia), as may be amended.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 “Affiliate ” means an entity associated with the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to the Business Corporations Act (British Columbia), the definition of 'Affiliate' becomes vague. This introduces uncertainty about what constitutes an affiliate, creating a contradiction if other sections rely on the legal definition for enforcement purposes. This is because 'associated with' has no legal defintion, while the original had a very strong defintion based in a pre-existing legal frame work
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.5 “Confidential Material ” means all information, in any form or medium , known or used by City or an Affiliate of the City which is not known to the general public, including, but not limited to, the know -how, trade secrets, strategic plans, technical information, product information, supplier information, customer information, financial information, marketing information and information as to business opportunities, methods and strategies and research and development of the City and its Affiliates. If and to the extent any Confidential Material is included in any report, ass essment, diagram, memorandum or other document or copied or reproduced in any other form or medium, such report, assessment, diagram, memorandum, document or Confidential Material in such other form or medium will be deemed to be Confidential Material.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined "Affiliate" with a clear legal definition (Business Corporations Act (British Columbia)). The changed text replaces this with "an entity associated with the Recipient," which lacks a specific legal meaning. The contradicted text (1.5) relies on the definition of "Affiliate of the City" to define "Confidential Material." Since the definition of Affiliate is no longer legally precise, it creates ambiguity and uncertainty on who the 'Affiliates of the City' are, which makes the scope of "Confidential Material" unclear. This means that the changed definition contradicts the contradicted text, which uses the original definition to determine what entities are included, and how that applies to confidential material.

perturbation.original_text: 1.4 “Recipient ’s Team ” means any person who is a member of the Recipient’s team, whether such member is an employee, sub- contractor or agent of the Recipient , or any employee or agent of such person.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 “Recipient ’s Team ” means individuals authorized by the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition clearly specifies employees, subcontractors, and agents. The modified definition, 'individuals authorized by the Recipient,' is broader and lacks clarity. It could include consultants, advisors, or even temporary staff not traditionally considered part of the team. This creates a contradiction if other sections assume the original definition when assigning responsibilities or liabilities. By omitting the detailed list of roles that can be included in the definition and switching it to a more general statement creates an in-text contradiction with the overall intentions of the definition of that term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 The Recipient will hold and keep, and will ensure that all of the Recipient ’s Team will hold and keep, the Confidential Material in confidence and in trust for the City, using at least the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as the Recipient uses to protect its own similar confidential information of like importance, and will, (a) prevent any access, reproduction, disclosure or use of the Confidential Material not expressly authorized herein, (b) disclose the Confidential Material only to those of the Recipient ’s Team who have a definable need to know such information for Purpose, provided that such Recipient ’s Team are bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Recipient no less restrictive than this Agreement, and in the event the employment or appointment of any such person is terminated, the Recipient agrees to use its best efforts to recover any of the Confidential Material in such person’s custody or control. The Recipient will be responsible for all damages arising from any disclosure of all or part of the Co nfidential Material or any act in contravention of this Agreement by a person to whom such Confidential Material was given by the Recipient as if the disclosure were made or the act performed directly by the Recipient , (c) not, and will ensure that each of the Recipient ’s Team will not, copy or reproduce any of the Confidential Material, except as strictly neces sary in order to carry out the Purpose, and (d) promptly notify the City in writing of any unauthorized copying, reproduction, use or disclosure of the Confidential Material of which the Recipient is or becomes aware, and such notice will include a detailed description of the circumstances of the copying, reproduction, use or disclosure and the parties involved.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Recipient’s Team' included specific roles like employees, subcontractors, and agents. The changed definition broadens it to 'individuals authorized by the Recipient' which lacks the original clarity. This creates a contradiction with section 3.3, which imposes specific obligations on the Recipient to ensure that all members of the Recipient's Team hold Confidential Material in confidence, are bound by confidentiality agreements, and that the Recipient uses best efforts to recover any Confidential Material in such person's custody upon termination of employment. The modified definition could include individuals who are not employees, subcontractors or agents, making it unclear if the same obligations in 3.3 apply, hence creating a direct conflict based on the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 The Recipient will use Confidential Material onl y as strictly required for the Purpose and only in the manner and upon the terms specified in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 The Recipient will use Confidential Material for the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'only as strictly required' and 'only in the manner and upon the terms specified in this Agreement,' the clause becomes significantly weaker. This creates a contradiction if other sections rely on a strict interpretation of usage limitations. The original clause was clear, while the new is subjective.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 The Recipient acknowledges and agrees with the City that (a) the secrecy of the Confidential Material is of the utmost importance to the City, and the Confidential Material is of a sensitive and confidential nature such that monetary damages alone may be inadequate to protect the City’s interests against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement (b) the covenants and conditions of this Agreement are reasonable and necessary for the protection of the City’s business and security and all defences to the s trict enforcement thereof by the City are hereby waived by the Recipient to the fullest extent permitted by law, and (c) a violation of any of the provisions of this Agreement will result in immediate and irreparable damage to the City, and so the City wil l, in addition to any other rights to relief, be entitled to the remedies of specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief in respect of any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, without proof of actual damages or the inadequacy of monetary damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the use of Confidential Material 'only as strictly required for the Purpose and only in the manner and upon the terms specified in this Agreement.' The changed text removes these restrictions, broadening the scope of permissible use. Section 7.1 emphasizes the 'utmost importance' of secrecy and strict enforcement of the agreement to protect the City's interests. By removing the strict usage limitations, the changed text contradicts the emphasis on secrecy and strict enforcement detailed in section 7.1, as the Recipient now has more leeway in how they use the Confidential Material, potentially weakening the City's protections and creating uncertainty in enforcement.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 Upon the request at any time of the City, the Recipient will promptly destroy all Confidential Material and any copies or reproductions thereof in the Recipient ’s possession or under its control or in the possession or under the control of any of the Recipient ’s Team , and will certify in writing such destruction or return of all Confidential Material. If compliance with the foregoing would violate any applicable law or regulation, then such information may be retained pr ovided that it is not used for any purpose other than to evidence the Recipient ’s compliance with such law or regulation, and such retained information must be maintained in confidence as set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 Upon the request at any time of the City, the Recipient will promptly return all Confidential Material. If compliance with the foregoing would violate any applicable law or regulation, then such information may be retained pr ovided that it is not used for any purpose other than to evidence the Recipient ’s compliance with such law or regulation, and such retained information must be maintained in confidence as set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'destroy all Confidential Material and any copies or reproductions thereof in the Recipient’s possession or under its control or in the possession or under the control of any of the Recipient ’s Team , and will certify in writing such destruction or return of all Confidential Material', the modified text introduces uncertainty about whether destruction is still an option. The removal contradicts the intention of the original NDA, which aimed to secure the City’s data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 The Recipient acknowledges and agrees with the City that (a) the secrecy of the Confidential Material is of the utmost importance to the City, and the Confidential Material is of a sensitive and confidential nature such that monetary damages alone may be inadequate to protect the City’s interests against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement (b) the covenants and conditions of this Agreement are reasonable and necessary for the protection of the City’s business and security and all defences to the s trict enforcement thereof by the City are hereby waived by the Recipient to the fullest extent permitted by law, and (c) a violation of any of the provisions of this Agreement will result in immediate and irreparable damage to the City, and so the City wil l, in addition to any other rights to relief, be entitled to the remedies of specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief in respect of any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, without proof of actual damages or the inadequacy of monetary damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for the destruction of confidential material, reinforcing the City's need for secrecy, as highlighted in contradicted text 7.1. The changed text removes the option for destruction, only requiring the return of the material. This creates a contradiction because simply returning the material may not adequately protect the City's interests if the Recipient retains copies or if the return doesn't guarantee the secrecy emphasized in 7.1. Therefore, the altered text weakens the initial agreement and may not prevent 'immediate and irreparable damage to the City,' as stated in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: 8.3 The Recipient will not, without the written consent of the City, disclose to any third party either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL NO. PS20131165 PRELIMINARY DESIGN OF THE GEORGIA RAMP AND STEPS NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT Page 1 concerning the Transactions or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to the Transactions including the status thereof or the subject matter of this Agreement, provided, however, that nothing herein will prevent the Recipient from making such di sclosure (a) on a confidential basis to any of the Recipient’s Team to the extent such person needs to know such information s trictly for the Purpose , or (b) in order to comply with the requirements of applicable securities or other laws.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.3 The Recipient will not, without the written consent of the City, disclose to any third party either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the Transactions or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to the Transactions including the status thereof or the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the exception clauses (a) and (b) for disclosure, the modified text strictly prohibits any disclosure to any third party, even the Recipient's Team or when required by law. This introduces a contradiction because it is unrealistic and potentially illegal to prevent disclosure to the Recipient's team for the purpose of completing the project, or prevent disclosure required by law. Omitting this causes in-text contradiction due to its unrealistic nature when compared to normal business functions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 The Recipient will hold and keep, and will ensure that all of the Recipient ’s Team will hold and keep, the Confidential Material in confidence and in trust for the City, using at least the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as the Recipient uses to protect its own similar confidential information of like importance, and will, (a) prevent any access, reproduction, disclosure or use of the Confidential Material not expressly authorized herein, (b) disclose the Confidential Material only to those of the Recipient ’s Team who have a definable need to know such information for Purpose, provided that such Recipient ’s Team are bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Recipient no less restrictive than this Agreement, and in the event the employment or appointment of any such person is terminated, the Recipient agrees to use its best efforts to recover any of the Confidential Material in such person’s custody or control. The Recipient will be responsible for all damages arising from any disclosure of all or part of the Co nfidential Material or any act in contravention of this Agreement by a person to whom such Confidential Material was given by the Recipient as if the disclosure were made or the act performed directly by the Recipient , (c) not, and will ensure that each of the Recipient ’s Team will not, copy or reproduce any of the Confidential Material, except as strictly neces sary in order to carry out the Purpose, and (d) promptly notify the City in writing of any unauthorized copying, reproduction, use or disclosure of the Confidential Material of which the Recipient is or becomes aware, and such notice will include a detailed description of the circumstances of the copying, reproduction, use or disclosure and the parties involved.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 8.3 allows for disclosure to the Recipient's Team and when required by law. The changed text removes these exceptions, creating a blanket prohibition on any disclosure. Section 3.3, the contradicted text, explicitly states the Recipient will ensure that all of the Recipient's Team will hold and keep the Confidential Material in confidence but also anticipates disclosure to the Recipient's Team for a definable need to know. The modified Section 8.3 directly contradicts Section 3.3, as it now prohibits the very disclosure that Section 3.3 requires be kept confidential but anticipates happening.
